label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user provided a letter from Walker to the president, and a question about the author's view on high school teachers. The options are A to D. I need to figure out which one is correct based on the letter. First, I'll reread the relevant part of the letter. The section about teachers says: "The high school students of today are America's future. In a way, America's future depends greatly on the high school teachers of America. Saying this, it is sad that teacher's salaries are where they are. While teachers are working harder and harder every day to teach high school students, CEO's of big companies that are driving our countries economy into the ground are getting bonuses. I'm not saying that teacher's salaries should be the same of CEO's of companies, but I do strongly believe that action should be taken to increase the salaries of teachers because they have one of the most important jobs in America - - preparing our country for the future." So the key points here are that the author thinks teacher salaries are too low ("it is sad that teacher's salaries are where they are") and that they should be increased. The author also contrasts this with CEOs getting bonuses, but explicitly says they're not suggesting teachers should earn the same as CEOs. The author emphasizes the importance of teachers' jobs in preparing the country's future but doesn't claim it's more important than all other jobs, just one of the most important. Looking at the options: A) the teachers' salaries are not high enough. This matches the statement about it being sad that salaries are where they are and that action should be taken to increase them. So A seems correct. B) teacher's salaries should be as high as CEO's. The author explicitly says they're not saying that, so B is incorrect. C) the teachers work harder than CEO's of companies. The letter mentions teachers are working harder every day but doesn't directly compare their work effort to CEOs. It's more about the contrast in compensation despite the importance of the job. So C might be a bit misleading. D) teaching is more important than any other job in America.
